spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates `corner-shape` as an animatable property via :hover transitions (round↔squircle, round↔bevel, squircle↔notch) and an `@keyframes` auto-cycle. The spec explicitly calls out animation as a headline capability.

interactive.rationale: CSS-driven hover interactivity drives 3 morphing cards; the auto-cycler runs a 6s @keyframes loop. For a CSS feature, CSS-only hover/animation qualifies per CLAUDE.md's CSS bullet.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises `hover cards morph round ↔ squircle, round ↔ bevel, squircle ↔ notch. Plus an auto-cycle @keyframes that sweeps four shapes on loop` — page is exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Three combinations covered but the spec also allows animating between values where one is `superellipse(n)` and the other is a named keyword — that transition's interpolation behaviour isn't shown. Also no demonstration of `prefers-reduced-motion` (animations should respect it).

references.rationale: `see also` contains only sibling pages — missing the chromestatus URL `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5357329815699456` required by invariant #3.

design_system.rationale: 36 CSS variable references, no raw hex, no button override mismatch.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: `see also` is missing `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5357329815699456`. Add it plus the css-borders-4 corner-shaping anchor.

openQuestions.title: Show animation between named keyword and superellipse(n)
openQuestions.detail: Add a fourth card that morphs from `superellipse(2)` to `superellipse(8)` (continuous N transition) and one that morphs from named `bevel` to `superellipse(0.5)` (mixed). The interpolation algorithm for mixed transitions is the spec's least-documented part.

openQuestions.title: Respect prefers-reduced-motion
openQuestions.detail: The auto-cycler runs forever. Wrap the animation in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` so users with reduced-motion preferences see a static state. This is best practice and the demo should model it.

openQuestions.title: Annotate the timing function used
openQuestions.detail: Different timing functions reveal the morph shape differently. Surface the `transition-timing-function` currently in use and consider adding a select to swap between `linear`, `ease`, `cubic-bezier(...)` so authors see the effect on the curve.

summary: Real CSS-only animation demo that proves `corner-shape` interpolates — exactly the spec's claim. Missing chromestatus link, doesn't respect prefers-reduced-motion, and skips the named↔superellipse mixed-transition case which is the trickiest part of the spec.
